Get a Bond

State‑by‑State Bond Requirement Guides

A complete resource designed to help businesses, contractors, and professionals understand the exact surety bond requirements in every U.S. state.

These guides break down licensing rules, bond amounts, renewal timelines, and regulatory obligations so you always know what your state requires before applying.

![](/assets/images/Learning Center Page-Surety Bond Basics Guide.png)

Why State‑Specific Guides Matter

Surety bond requirements vary widely across states. Bond amounts, licensing rules, and compliance expectations can differ even within the same industry. These guides give you a clear, organized way to navigate those differences and avoid delays or compliance issues.

What Each State Guide Includes

Bond Amounts & Required Bond Types

Each guide outlines the mandatory bond amounts for major industries, including:

License & permit bonds

Contractor and construction bonds

Auto dealer bonds

Agriculture & food bonds

Freight broker and transportation bonds

Professional and occupational bonds

You’ll know exactly which bond you need and how much coverage is required.

Licensing & Application Requirements

Every state has its own licensing process. Each guide includes:

Required forms and documentation

Application steps

Background or credit check requirements

State agency contact information

Filing instructions

This helps you prepare a complete application the first time.

Renewal & Compliance Timelines

To help you stay compliant year‑round, each guide provides:

Bond renewal deadlines

License renewal cycles

Reporting and filing requirements

Ongoing compliance obligations

These timelines prevent lapses that could lead to penalties or license suspension.

Regulatory Agencies by State

Each guide lists the agencies responsible for regulating your industry, such as:

Departments of Insurance

Departments of Agriculture

Motor Vehicle Divisions

Contractor Licensing Boards

Public Utilities Commissions

You’ll always know who oversees your bond and license.

Industry‑Specific Notes & Exceptions

1Some states have unique rules or higher bond amounts for certain industries. Each guide highlights:

State‑specific exceptions

Additional compliance requirements

Special endorsements or filings

This ensures you’re aware of any extra steps before applying.

Who These Guides Are For

These guides are ideal for:

New businesses applying for their first license

Companies expanding into multiple states

Contractors working across state lines

Auto dealers, freight brokers, and agricultural businesses

Compliance teams and licensing specialists

How to Use These Guides

Select your state.

Review the bond amounts and licensing requirements.

Follow the checklist to prepare your application.

Use the renewal timelines to stay compliant.

These guides simplify the entire process and help you meet every requirement with confidence.

YOU ARE RESPONSIBLE FOR DETERMINING WHAT INSURANCE PRODUCTS YOU NEED AND IN WHAT AMOUNTS, BASED ON YOUR UNIQUE EXPOSURE TO RISKS AND ABILITY TO BEAR LOSSES. Stephen Wright is a licensed insurance agent in Arizona. Insurance products and features are subject to underwriting criteria and may not be available in all states.
